title: Automated watering system
abstract: A watering system including a housing, a platform rotatable above the housing in a degree of azimuth, brackets extending upward from the platform, a nozzle mounted between the brackets rotatable in a degree of elevation, a first motor and gears configured to rotate the platform, a second motor and gears configured to rotate the nozzle, and a processor mounted in the housing controlling activation of the first and second motors, wherein the second motor gears include first and second meshed bevel gears, wherein the first bevel gear is rotated parallel to the platform, and independent of the platform rotation, and the second bevel gear is attached to the platform and is translated with the platform rotation, whereby the second bevel gear rotates about the center of the platform according to a differential between the rotation of the platform and the rotation of the first bevel gear.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A watering system comprising: a stationary housing; a platform mounted above said housing with a first rolling bearing to enable rotation in a degree of azimuth; upright brackets extending upward from said platform; a nozzle mounted between said brackets with a second rolling bearing to enable rotation in a degree of elevation, wherein the center of the nozzle is on the same line that is perpendicular to the elevation rotation axis; a first motor mounted in said housing; a first gear system connecting said first motor to said platform, configured to rotate said platform in a degree of azimuth; a second motor mounted in said housing; a second gear system connecting said second motor to said nozzle configured to rotate said nozzle in a degree of elevation, comprising first and second meshed bevel gears, wherein the first bevel gear is rotated parallel to said platform, and independent of the platform rotation, by said second motor, wherein the second bevel gear is attached to said platform and is translated with the platform rotation, whereby the second bevel gear rotates about the center of said platform according to a differential between the rotation of said platform and the rotation of the first bevel gear, wherein rotation of said nozzle in a degree of elevation is driven by said second bevel gear. 2. The watering system of claim 1, further comprising a processor mounted in said housing controlling activation of said first and second motors. 3. The watering system of claim 2, wherein at least one gear in said first gear system and at least one gear in said second gear system have the same pitch diameter to facilitate said processor to track the azimuth and elevation angles of said nozzle based on rotations of said first and second motors. 4. The watering system of claim 3, wherein said second gear system further comprises: a first timing pulley or sprocket wheel rotated by said meshed bevel gears; a second timing pulley or sprocket wheel rotating said nozzle; and a timing belt running over said first and second sprocket wheels. 5. The watering system of claim 4, wherein a ratio of rotations between said first and second meshed bevel gears is equal to a ratio of rotations between said second sprocket and said first sprocket. 6. The watering system of claim 3, further comprising a pipe, for transmitting water from a water source to said nozzle, extending through the center of said platform, the pipe comprising: a first pipe portion in said housing; and a second pipe portion above said platform connected to said nozzle in a manner configured to allow said nozzle to rotate around an axis at the center of the second pipe portion's cross section, wherein the 

second portion is configured to rotate around an axis at the center of the first portion's cross section. 7. The watering system of claim 6, further comprising: a poppet valve connected to an inlet to said first pipe portion, comprising a movable stem regulating flow pressure through the valve and into said pipe, wherein stem movement is controlled by said processor; and a pressure sensor attached to said first pipe portion near an outlet of said poppet valve, measuring flow pressure through said pipe, and providing flow pressure feedback to said processor. 8. The watering system of claim 7, wherein said first pipe portion and said pressure sensor are configured to enable said pressure sensor to be repeatedly attached to and detached from said first pipe portion. 9. The watering system of claim 7, wherein said poppet valve is configured such that there is a substantially linear relationship between the distance traveled by said stem and the resulting change in pressure. 10. The watering system of claim 6, wherein said nozzle has an elongated shape and comprises: an cylindrical outlet at one end of the nozzle; a seal at the other end of the nozzle; an inlet along a side of said nozzle between said outlet and said seal, receiving water from said second pipe portion; a plurality of parallel tubes inside the nozzle each parallel tube extending from above said seal to below said outlet, whereby water entering said nozzle through said inlet travels toward said seal, into the 

 parallel tubes and out said outlet in a laminar flow generated by the parallel tubes. 11. The watering system of claim 10, wherein inner walls of said nozzle above said parallel tubes are smooth to reduce turbulence for water flowing out of said nozzle. 12. The watering system of claim 10, wherein said parallel tubes vary in at least one of size, shape and length, to achieve an even speed of water flowing through said parallel tubes, to enhance the laminar effect. 13. The watering system of claim 6, further comprising a barrier between said platform and said housing preventing water above said platform from entering into said housing. 14. The watering system of claim 6, wherein said housing is configured for modular attachment and detachment from said platform. 15. The watering system of claim 6, wherein said platform is a portable fixture. 16. The watering system of claim 6, wherein said platform is attached to a stake inserted into the ground. 17. The watering system of claim 6, wherein said housing and said platform are configured for modular insertion and removal from a cavity equipped with a water source. 18. The watering system of claim 6, configured to enable multiple revolutions of said platform in the azimuth direction and multiple revolutions of said nozzle in the elevation direction. 19. The watering system of claim 6, characterized in that there are no electrical wires above said platform.
